jokes.text: Oh, is that code for some kind of older gal medical procedure?
jokes.setup: Character mentions having lunch with boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent misinterpretation as medical euphemism
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth naivety - perfectly in character and well-structured misdirection

jokes.text: Well, don't get too attached. As soon as people realize his tonics don't work it's on to the next town.
jokes.setup: Mention that boyfriend is a doctor
jokes.punchline: Kenneth assumes he's a traveling snake oil salesman
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth worldview - assumes all doctors are 1800s charlatans. Perfect character consistency and unexpected absurdity

jokes.text: Catalog photos of expectant mothers in their swimming suits?
jokes.setup: Character asking for jerk chicken and something else
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's disturbing completion of the order
jokes.explanation: Deeply weird and specific - perfectly captures Kenneth's unsettling omniscience about his boss's bizarre preferences

jokes.text: You're my Radar O'Reilly, Ken. Now get in here and rub my feet till you hear a chopper coming.
jokes.setup: Comparing Kenneth to MASH character Radar
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of the helicopter reference
jokes.explanation: Clever reference joke that works on multiple levels - both M*A*S*H reference and Tracy's entitled demands

jokes.text: Oh, upbeat and confused.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about Tracy's mood
jokes.punchline: Perfectly deadpan summary of Tracy's mental state
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical character description that captures both Tracy's personality and Liz's weary familiarity with it

jokes.text: I'm thinking about some of them now. / Me too.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions crazy things they've done with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Both characters awkwardly remembering specific incidents
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's own history - works for longtime viewers who know what crazy things they're referencing

jokes.text: His video game made a fortune and he invested all of it in a company that dismantles bank signs. They're doing very well.
jokes.setup: Tracy's not about money anymore because his investments
jokes.punchline: Completely random but apparently profitable business
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd - the kind of random successful business only 30 Rock would invent. 'They're doing very well' is perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You guys are best friends forever? / That's not what that stands for.
jokes.setup: Liz assumes B.F.F. means best friends forever
jokes.punchline: Jack's ominous correction implying something darker
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience assumes innocent meaning, Jack's response suggests something sinister. Economy is exceptional

jokes.text: Why would you celebrate that?
jokes.setup: Jack's implication that B.F.F. means something awful
jokes.punchline: Liz's horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up reaction that heightens the previous joke's discomfort

jokes.text: Excuse me. I'm Calvin Klein and I think you should be my next underwear model. / Whoa, thanks! / You don't wanna get a card or... a phone number? / He'll figure it out.
jokes.setup: Random person claims to be Calvin Klein and offers modeling contract
jokes.punchline: Drew accepts it completely at face value with no verification
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Drew's bubble - shows how good-looking people get absurd opportunities and never question them

jokes.text: Mayor Bloomberg asked him to dance.
jokes.setup: List of special treatment Drew receives
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unexpected final item
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation in a list of perks - starts normal (free food, drinks) and ends with bizarrely specific Bloomberg reference

jokes.text: They live in a bubble... a bubble of free drinks, kindness and outdoor sex.
jokes.setup: Beautiful people live in a bubble
jokes.punchline: Unexpected escalation to 'outdoor sex'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - starts with innocent perks and ends somewhere unexpectedly sexual

jokes.text: Jack, I want you to pay close attention to the following over-the-top eye-roll. Oh, brother.
jokes.setup: Liz announces she's about to do an eye-roll
jokes.punchline: The announced and performed eye-roll
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly meta - announcing the physical comedy before doing it. Very Liz Lemon to overthink even a simple gesture

jokes.text: What the what?! You have a Superman chest!
jokes.setup: Jack shows photo of himself at 25
jokes.punchline: Liz's shocked reaction to his physique
jokes.explanation: 'What the what' is classic Liz catchphrase, and 'Superman chest' is a funny specific description

jokes.text: Oh, my God, the lady will have two tickets to the gun show!
jokes.setup: Continuing reaction to Jack's muscular photo
jokes.punchline: Liz using masculine 'gun show' reference awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Somewhat predictable 'gun show' reference but works for Liz's character - trying too hard to be casual/masculine

jokes.text: You look just how I picture Mary Magdalene. / You're what I think Judas looks like.
jokes.setup: Kenneth comparing Jenna to Mary Magdalene
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's brutal comparison of someone else to Judas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - innocent religious comparisons that are accidentally brutal. The contrast between compliment and insult is perfect

jokes.text: Well, because, Kenneth, I am a selfless person who can't get arrested in this town.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she's cutting her hair for charity
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's actually about getting attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - claiming to be selfless while immediately contradicting herself. 'Can't get arrested' is good showbiz idiom

jokes.text: Sorry it took me so long to answer. I was just thinking about how weird it is that we eat birds.
jokes.setup: Tracy taking time to respond to contract offer
jokes.punchline: His mind was on completely random philosophical thought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan randomness - his mind goes to completely unrelated philosophical musings during important business. Wonderfully absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: Tracy's is a tactile-kinesthetic learning style... Dotcom... so help me God.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to explain Tracy's thought process academically
jokes.punchline: Breaking into threatening tone mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jack's corporate speak breaking down into genuine frustration. The academic terminology makes the threat funnier

jokes.text: It's nice of those guys to give up their court for us. / Yes... for us.
jokes.setup: Liz comments on getting tennis court
jokes.punchline: Drew's oblivious agreement missing the sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Good bubble example - Drew doesn't realize the court wasn't given up voluntarily, misses Liz's sarcasm entirely

jokes.text: So I should probably warn you, I was pro in college.
jokes.setup: Drew warning Liz about his tennis skills
jokes.punchline: Sets up expectation that will be completely subverted
jokes.explanation: Setup for later payoff - establishes false expectation about Drew's abilities

jokes.text: Excuse me... do you give lessons?
jokes.setup: Someone watches Drew play tennis terribly
jokes.punchline: Still wants lessons because of his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect bubble moment - person ignores obvious lack of skill because of attraction

jokes.text: What is this orange-y taste? / Gatorade.
jokes.setup: Liz tasting something odd in fancy dish
jokes.punchline: Drew used sports drink as cooking ingredient
jokes.explanation: Perfect bubble behavior - Drew thinks he can cook but makes terrible choices. The casual 'Gatorade' delivery is perfect

jokes.text: Are you choking? Are you choking? I'm a doctor. Stay calm, stay calm. I just need... I just need to shake it loose.
jokes.setup: Someone appears to be choking
jokes.punchline: Doctor's completely wrong 'medical' response
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - doctor who doesn't know basic first aid because he's never had to actually help anyone

jokes.text: Oh no, he's not a B.F.F.? / Ugh, no! Never!
jokes.setup: Jack asking about Drew using the earlier mysterious B.F.F. acronym
jokes.punchline: Liz's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that reminds us of the earlier dark B.F.F. implication

jokes.text: He is a doctor who doesn't know the Heimlich Maneuver. He can't play tennis, he can't cook... he's as bad at sex as I am. But he has no idea!
jokes.setup: List of things Drew is bad at
jokes.punchline: Unexpected self-deprecating admission about sex
jokes.explanation: Great Liz moment - starts as criticism of Drew, accidentally reveals her own insecurities. Good escalation and character consistency

jokes.text: For years... I thought I spoke excellent French.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining dangers of the bubble
jokes.punchline: His own bubble experience with language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack vanity moment - implies people let him butcher French for years. The pause before the admission is key

jokes.text: You wake a sleepwalker, you risk getting urinated on. / Or thanked... on.
jokes.setup: Warning about waking sleepwalkers
jokes.punchline: Jack's awkward attempt to make it sound positive
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that almost works - Jack trying to spin the metaphor positively but it comes out wrong

jokes.text: Oh, no! When I get upset my accent come out. And when it gets to comin' out, I can't get to talkin', nuh-uh!
jokes.setup: Kenneth getting upset about Tracy situation
jokes.punchline: His accent getting stronger as he talks about it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth moment - his accent literally getting thicker as he describes the problem. Self-fulfilling prophecy is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Don't cling to this, Lemon. Be a manager. Control your people. Buy better clothes.
jokes.setup: Jack giving Liz management advice
jokes.punchline: Unexpected personal insult tacked on
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - mixing business advice with personal criticism. The clothing comment comes from nowhere and lands perfectly

jokes.text: Nope, I lost it, that was Batman.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to do a voice impression
jokes.punchline: Accidentally did Batman instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth randomness - his earnest admission that he accidentally did Batman voice. Completely out of nowhere but in character

jokes.text: Before 'The Rachel,' Jennifer Aniston was just a chunky nobody who couldn't get a job. / Wasn't she already on Friends? / Richard Esposito, move to the back.
jokes.setup: Jenna's false claim about Jennifer Aniston
jokes.punchline: Writer corrects her, Jenna banishes him
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna moment - making up show business facts and punishing anyone who corrects her. Very diva behavior

jokes.text: Everyone shout out words that describe my beauty. / Fading. / '80s. / 1880s.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking for flattering descriptions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly brutal and specific insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - starts with basic insult (fading), moves to era ('80s), then gets historically specific (1880s). Great structure

jokes.text: I want you to come back to the T.G.S. for the people who like the jokes and the things.
jokes.setup: Fake Cosby trying to convince Tracy
jokes.punchline: Vague, bad Cosby impression dialogue
jokes.explanation: Decent impression joke - the vagueness of 'jokes and things' captures bad celebrity impersonation

jokes.text: Bill Cosby, you got a lot of nerve getting on the phone with me after what you did to my Aunt Paulette. / 1971, Cincinnati. She was the cocktail waitress with the droopy eye.
jokes.setup: Tracy angry at fake Cosby
jokes.punchline: Specific, bizarre backstory about his aunt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy randomness - creates entire fictional history with Cosby including specific details. 'Droopy eye' detail makes it perfect

jokes.text: Dr. Heathcliff Huxtable... with your light-ass kids!
jokes.setup: Tracy's continued anger at fake Cosby
jokes.punchline: Mixing real person with fictional character and random complaint
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy confusion - conflating actor with character and complaining about fictional children's skin tone

jokes.text: Tracy, this is Billy Dee Williams. I just wanna say I love your work. It's very smooth.
jokes.setup: Jack switching to different celebrity impression
jokes.punchline: Generic compliment with Billy Dee's signature word 'smooth'
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to failed Cosby strategy, uses Billy Dee's known catchphrase

jokes.text: Mayonnaise... and a pack of cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Showing what Tracy packed for son's lunch
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate 'lunch' items
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Tracy's incompetence as domestic figure - gives child condiment and cigarettes as meal

jokes.text: # My girl has a fat neck? I'm sharp, let's do it again.
jokes.setup: Tracy set up home recording studio
jokes.punchline: His terrible song lyrics and demanding attitude
jokes.explanation: Hilariously bad song lyrics combined with Tracy's unwarranted confidence. 'Fat neck' is so specific and wrong

jokes.text: He set up a home recording studio in our rec room! 'My girl has a fat neck? I'm sharp, let's do it again.'
jokes.setup: More complaints about Tracy's home behavior
jokes.punchline: Offensive song lyrics Tracy is recording
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's creative process at home - inappropriate and repetitive

jokes.text: I came here to see the dumbest cracker in all of New York.
jokes.setup: Tracy Jr. explaining why he came to NBC
jokes.punchline: Brutal, specific insult about Jack
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that's specific to location and character dynamic. Gets at Jack's corporate cluelessness

jokes.text: I came here to see the dumbest cracker in all of New York.
jokes.setup: Tracy Jr. explaining why he came to see Jack
jokes.punchline: Blunt racial insult combined with specific geographic superlative
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult with specific NYC framing, shows family attitude toward Jack

jokes.text: You try to order off the menu again I will smack those glasses off your face.
jokes.setup: Drew trying to order special items
jokes.punchline: Waitress's threatening response to normal person behavior
jokes.explanation: Good bubble-bursting moment - shows how service workers actually treat difficult customers when beauty privilege is removed

jokes.text: Actually, they used footage of me from my high school swim team to draw Prince Eric.
jokes.setup: Liz comparing Drew to Disney prince
jokes.punchline: Drew casually revealing he actually was the model
jokes.explanation: Perfect bubble moment - what seems like hyperbole turns out to be literal truth. Completely absurd but delivered casually

jokes.text: Oh, yes, you can. The hot Italian lady from the Food Network told me so. Did she say it on TV? No, she said it to me when she jumped escalators to try to talk...
jokes.setup: Drew defending his Gatorade cooking
jokes.punchline: Reveals celebrity chased him to give cooking advice
jokes.explanation: Great bubble moment showing how even bad advice becomes credible when delivered by attractive people

jokes.text: That's it! I quit! This racket is a fart and you cheat! You're a cheating bitch!
jokes.setup: Drew experiencing reality of losing at tennis
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown with childish insults
jokes.explanation: Great bubble-bursting payoff - Drew's first real loss causes complete breakdown. 'This racket is a fart' is wonderfully childish

jokes.text: That's it! I quit! This racket is a fart and you cheat! You're a cheating bitch!
jokes.setup: Drew losing at tennis when treated fairly
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown with childish insults and equipment blame
jokes.explanation: Perfect bubble-bursting moment with Drew's complete inability to handle fair competition

jokes.text: I know Mr. Jordan like the back of my stepfather Ron's hand.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he knows Tracy well
jokes.punchline: Twisted version of common expression revealing family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth line - takes familiar phrase and reveals dark family history with perfect economy. Implies abuse while being innocent

jokes.text: I know that by 'binky' he means 1970s pornography.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing things he knows about Tracy
jokes.punchline: Innocent term for disturbing adult content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth inappropriate innocence - using baby term for adult content and being very specific about the decade

jokes.text: I know that by 'binky' he means 1970s pornography.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing things he knows about Tracy
jokes.punchline: Innocent delivery of inappropriate information about Tracy's habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence delivering disturbing information about Tracy's specific pornography preferences

jokes.text: Hello, Studio 6-H. This is... Cranston.
jokes.setup: Kenneth answering phone with fake name
jokes.punchline: His awkward pause before obviously fake name
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment - his inability to lie convincingly. The pause sells his discomfort with deception

jokes.text: # Fat-necked girl, let me count your neck rings?
jokes.setup: Hearing more of Tracy's terrible song
jokes.punchline: Even worse lyrics about counting neck rings
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the terrible song with even worse lyrics. The tree ring metaphor is absurdly specific

jokes.text: Plus, I've started to dream as Cranston.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why the plan is too hard
jokes.punchline: His fake identity is taking over his subconscious
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth escalation - his innocent nature can't handle even minor deception to the point it invades his dreams. Perfect absurdist logic

jokes.text: But this is for a wonderful charity called Merkins of Hope.
jokes.setup: Jenna naming her alternative charity
jokes.punchline: The sexual wig pun name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'Locks of Love.' Merkins are pubic wigs, making this appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, no, Locks of Love turned me down. They said my hair was too processed for a sick person to wear.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming to donate hair to charity
jokes.punchline: Actual charity rejected her hair as too damaged
jokes.explanation: Great Jenna moment revealing her 'selfless' gesture was already rejected by real charity

jokes.text: Oh, no, Locks of Love turned me down. They said my hair was too processed for a sick person to wear.
jokes.setup: Talking about hair donation charity
jokes.punchline: Jenna's hair is too damaged even for cancer patients
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - even her charitable gesture is rejected for being too vain. The specificity of 'too processed' is brutal

jokes.text: But this is for a wonderful charity called Merkins of Hope.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her alternative charity
jokes.punchline: Fake charity name that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Decent pun on 'Locks of Love' but using term for pubic wigs. Fits Jenna's clueless sexuality

jokes.text: I guess I'm only farm-strong, and not heart-strong.
jokes.setup: Kenneth apologizing for giving in to Tracy
jokes.punchline: His made-up distinction between types of strength
jokes.explanation: Sweet Kenneth moment - his folksy way of admitting emotional weakness. The made-up terminology is very him

jokes.text: Of course you don't, you idiot. I'm coming back to work, Jack... with Kenneth.
jokes.setup: Tracy seeming to understand Jack's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's outsmarted Jack and gets what he wants
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Tracy shows he understood the game and played it better than Jack

jokes.text: I want to use 'ironic' however I want.
jokes.setup: Liz correcting Drew's word usage
jokes.punchline: Drew's defiant defense of incorrect word usage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Drew choosing ignorance over correction - wants bubble back

jokes.text: Or as the French say... / Yeah. I'll see you around.
jokes.setup: Drew attempting to use French farewell
jokes.punchline: Complete failure followed by Liz's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Jack's French bubble experience. Drew's failure and Liz's flat response close the bubble theme well
